title: Cat Squinting Left Eye: Causes, Triage, and Care Tips
meta_desc: Is your cat squinting their left eye? Learn about common causes, urgency levels, and how to help your vet with triage photos.
content_html:
What Does Squinting in One Eye Mean?

When your cat squints their left eye, it is a clinical sign known as blepharospasm. This is usually a response to pain, discomfort, or light sensitivity. In many cases, it indicates that the eyelid is reacting to a minor irritation, such as a dust particle, a small scratch on the cornea, or a mild allergic reaction. It is the body's natural way of protecting the globe of the eye from further irritation.

Is It an Emergency?

The urgency level for a cat squinting a single eye with no other symptoms is generally classified as Low. While eye issues should never be ignored, if your cat is still eating, playing, and does not have significant swelling or thick discharge, you can typically monitor them for a few hours. However, if the squinting persists for more than 24 hours, or if you notice the eye turning cloudy, a veterinary exam is necessary to rule out infections or ulcers.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

Providing your veterinarian or triage assistant with a clear, high-resolution photo of the affected eye can significantly speed up the assessment process. A photo allows a professional to look for subtle signs of redness, discharge type, or foreign objects without the cat being actively stressed by a physical exam. It also serves as a baseline to determine if the condition is improving or worsening over time.

faqs.question: Can I use human eye drops on my cat?
faqs.answer: No, you should never use human medications or over-the-counter eye drops on a cat unless specifically directed by a veterinarian, as some ingredients can be toxic or worsen certain conditions.

faqs.question: What are common signs of eye irritation in cats?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squinting, excessive tearing, redness of the white part of the eye, pawing at the face, and a visible third eyelid.

faqs.question: How should I clean my cat's eye?
faqs.answer: If there is mild discharge, you can gently wipe the area around the eye with a soft cloth dampened with warm water. Do not touch the eyeball itself.
